description_long: A cluster of four wooden barrels is shown, each constructed from vertical greenish wooden staves bound by three wide, grayish metal hoops. The barrels have a weathered, mossy appearance, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure, and the wood grain is clearly visible, adding to their rustic character. The tops and bottoms are flat and made of the same wood, with no visible openings or lids, and one barrel is lying on its side, revealing the circular end. The barrels are tightly grouped, with some slight irregularity in their alignment, enhancing the natural, hand-crafted look. The metal bands are evenly spaced and appear slightly corroded, further emphasizing their age and use. No handles, labels, or decorative markings are present, and the barrels are hollow inside, as seen from the open tops.
description: A group of four weathered, greenish wooden barrels with gray metal bands, showing signs of age and outdoor exposure. The barrels are tightly clustered, with one lying on its side and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Four green wooden barrels with gray metal bands, tightly grouped, all standing upright with open tops visible.
description_view_1: The barrels are seen from an angle showing one barrel lying on its side, exposing its circular end, while the others remain upright.
description_view_2: The side view highlights the horizontal orientation of one barrel and the curvature of the metal bands on all barrels.
description_view_3: The barrels are viewed from behind, with the open end of the horizontal barrel partially visible and the upright barrels forming a staggered row.
